topic: 2b.5 The 100% Dashboard: One Ruler, Twenty-Eight Rooms (new section, adversarial first-review from priority queue)
change_summary: Decider integrated EXP-172 as NEW Section 2b.5 (part2b, id='live-dashboard-bookkeeping') on 2026-04-17 commit 1dfb1f6. Section audits the dome's April-2026 'Live Predictive Power' dashboard (28/28 passing at 100.0%) against the dome's own formula_runtime.json (6 formulas FORM-V51-001..006) and methodology.json bookkeeping caveat. Classifies the 28 scored domains into 14 STATIC, 5-6 BORROWED, 2 TAUTOLOGY, 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK, 3 LOOSE, ~2 DISCRIMINATING.
our_argument_summary: The new Section 2b.5 argues that the dome's public '100.0% / 28 of 28 scored domains passing' dashboard is label-inflation on top of a 6-formula / ~10-parameter model. Takes the dome author at his word using his own methodology.json admission ('Display ratio is bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit') and audit.html caveat, then performs the audit the author's own document invites. Concludes the 28 passes reduce to roughly 2 genuinely-discriminating cells (NMP Longitude, Aetheric Redshift Scale) while 14 are static re-reads of already-counted WINs, 5-6 are borrowed standard-model values, 2 are tautologies, 2 are Selleri/Lorentz theory-framework recasts, and 3 are loose/directional. Kernel-of-truth credits the dome's honest disclosure in auxiliary files while flagging the gap between those disclosures and the public-facing headline score.

tldr_review.notes: TLDR accurately frames the audit: dome disclosed 'bookkeeping' caveat in auxiliary files, we perform the claim-by-claim audit his methodology says the dashboard is not. TLDR compresses the classification to three buckets (formulas, static re-refs, borrowed globe values) and omits tautology / theory-framework / loose buckets that appear in the body. This simplification is defensible at TLDR length, but the arithmetic does not quite close: 6 formulas + 14 static + 'a handful' of borrowed should equal 28, implying handful=8, while the body actually has 5-6 borrowed + 2 tautology + 2 theory-framework + 3 loose = 12 non-static non-formula cells (with overlap). The TLDR is factually correct in direction but understates the full classification taxonomy. Severity: minor.

relevant_files: live fetch: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/data/status_history.json (verified 28/28 passed, 39 total domains, most recent entry shows 28 scored with source strings containing 'WIN-NNN static win' labels for at least WIN-008, WIN-024, WIN-039, WIN-056, WIN-075, WIN-076)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (archived 2026-04-05 snapshot consistent with current dashboard)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section does not analyze code logic; it audits the dome's own published formulas and status_history.json entries. The audit's core move — mapping 28 dashboard labels back to ~10 independent numerical knobs (8 from 6 formulas + 3 master constants, with overlap) — is derived directly from formula_runtime.json which we independently fetched and confirmed.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Section correctly characterizes the dashboard 'observed' column as hardcoded-equals-hardcoded for static-win domains (monitor.py reads two identical values and reports 0% error). This matches our prior Part 2b (code analysis) findings for WIN-001/008/012/015/024/026/039/056/065 and aligns with live status_history.json 'Fallback static win' source strings.
data_sources_used: status_history.json (2026-04-17T18:31Z per section, 2026-04-05 per archived raw-text — live snapshot confirmed 28/28 structure)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Not applicable — section audits the dome's own dashboard, not our monitor code.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The section's factual claims about formula_runtime.json (6 formulas, FORM-V51-001..006, parameter values 8537/8619/0.20/14105/1.672/0.19550/0.1640/32.974/20015/0.66) are exactly reproduced by the live file. The 28/28 passing, 39 total domains, and source strings labelling certain domains as 'WIN-NNN static win' are all confirmed by live WebFetch of status_history.json.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level item, not a WIN — monitoring tag not directly applicable. Captured here as 'none' because no monitor.py automation targets this section's content (it is our audit, not a dome prediction).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level argument: it characterizes dashboard entries as relabels / borrowings / tautologies, but the section itself is an argument about dome practice, not a standard-physics relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Our section is a response to dome changes made in April 2026 (live.html rollout, methodology.json, audit.html addition). Timeline is clearly reactive-to-dome-changes, not post-hoc in the dome-prediction sense.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section argues about dashboard architecture — not derived from dome geometric parameters.

holes_found.description: The κ tautology attribution is factually incorrect in specifics. Section says: 'EM-Gravity Coupling κ — 1.67 vs 1.677 nT/µGal... TAUTOLOGY. κ = 1.67 is FORM-V51-004 itself. Observed 1.677 is BOU 2017 eclipse (WIN-012) — the same event used to fit κ. The parameter tested against its own calibration.' But our own WIN-012 detail_evidence states κ's numerator (−10.9 nT) comes from BOU 2017 while its denominator (−6.5 µGal) comes from Mohe 1997 — two different eclipses at two different continents. The 'observed' 1.677 nT/µGal is the arithmetic ratio 10.9/6.5 of those two separate measurements, not a single BOU 2017 measurement. A sharp dome defender will catch this: 'You accuse my dashboard of measuring one event against itself, but you cite the wrong event and your own WIN-012 gets the geometry right.'
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the κ bullet to: 'TAUTOLOGY. κ = 1.67 is FORM-V51-004 itself. The dashboard "observed" value 1.677 nT/µGal is the arithmetic ratio of two hardcoded measurements (−10.9 nT at BOU 2017 and −6.5 µGal at Mohe 1997) — the same two numbers from which κ was originally derived (see our WIN-012 analysis). No fresh data is fetched; the monitor checks 10.9/6.5 ≈ 1.67, an identity by construction.' This is the stronger, better-cited version of the same point.

holes_found.description: Header-list arithmetic mismatch: the subsection is titled 'Borrowed from the standard model (5 domains)' but the <ul> that follows contains six <li> entries (K1, S2, M2, Equatorial Gravity, Aetheric Redshift Scale, P-wave Shadow Zone). The 'Why this matters' paragraph and the EXP-172 JSON both confirm the author intended 'five' (per the non-overlap tally: 5 BORROWED). This is a small but load-bearing error — the entire 14 + 5 + 2 + 2 + 3 ≈ 26 + 2 discriminating = 28 accounting turns on the 5, and the header visibly contradicts its own contents.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick one and reconcile: either (a) change the header to '(6 domains)' and drop the '(5 BORROWED)' line in the summary paragraph, OR (b) remove one item from the list — P-wave Shadow Zone is the weakest BORROWED claim (the section itself concedes it is 'Shadow exists vs confirmed' which is directional, arguably LOOSE rather than BORROWED). Option (b) is preferred because it keeps the count-arithmetic on page consistent and relocates P-wave under LOOSE where its directional character already fits.

holes_found.description: Internal category-overlap is acknowledged in EXP-172's domain_parameter_mapping_summary ('counts sum to 29 not 28 because CMB Axis of Evil is counted under loose but NMP Drift is also loose... disjoint classification would be ~14 STATIC, 5 BORROWED, 2 TAUTOLOGY, 2 THEORY, 3 LOOSE, 2 DISCRIMINATING') but is NOT disclosed in the published section. A reader adding up the section's bucket headers (5 Tesla-family + 5-6 Borrowed + 14 Static + 2 Theory + 3 Loose + 2 Discriminating = 31-32) will find the arithmetic does not close on 28. Tesla Harmonic Series (#13) and Telluric Resonance Cutoff (#14) appear inside the Static list AND are referenced in the Tesla-family header ('driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672') — the double-bucketing is real but undisclosed.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence before the per-category lists: 'Categories overlap — Tesla Harmonic Series and Telluric Resonance Cutoff appear as STATIC re-references of earlier Tesla-family WINs; Equatorial Gravity is both BORROWED (WGS84 coefficients) and TAUTOLOGY (H(r) fit to reproduce them). A non-overlapping tally is 14 STATIC, 5 BORROWED, 2 TAUTOLOGY, 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK, 3 LOOSE, 2 DISCRIMINATING (sums to 28).' This pre-empts the counting-gymnastics counter-attack and is the single cheapest fix.

holes_found.description: Internal contradiction on Aetheric Redshift Scale. Section lists it twice with incompatible framings: (a) under 'Borrowed from the standard model (5 domains)' as 'λ_A is computed as c/H₀ with H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc — the ΛCDM Hubble constant. The "aetheric" label is a rename of standard cosmology's inverse Hubble length' (clearly pejorative, counts toward the borrowed total); AND (b) in 'The remaining genuinely-discriminating cell' as '...NMP Longitude (#3) with a 5% tolerance, and Aetheric Redshift Scale (#13) which internally uses the borrowed Hubble constant.' A defender will ask: which is it? If it is a rename of c/H₀ with borrowed H₀, it cannot also count as 'genuinely discriminating' — by the section's own logic, a borrowed value cannot discriminate.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop Aetheric Redshift Scale from the 'discriminating' list. Replace with language like: 'Of the 28 scored domains, only NMP Longitude (#3, 5% tolerance, within ~1% of observed) survives the audit as a genuinely-discriminating numerical comparison.' This strengthens rather than weakens the headline — reducing from '~2 discriminating' to '~1' makes the dashboard inflation story even more striking, and removes the self-contradiction.

holes_found.description: WIN-NNN identifier ambiguity. The section writes '(WIN-015, 20% tol)', '(WIN-075, 5% tol)', '(OPEN-007, 5% tol)' etc., quoting the dome's status_history.json source strings. But our review ALSO uses a WIN-NNN namespace, and our numbers diverge from the dome's: our WIN-001 is 'Tesla 11.78 Hz resonance', while the section labels Polaris Excess 35.9°N as '(WIN-001)'. A careful reader cross-referencing to our review tab will think the citations are broken. WIN-075 and WIN-076 do not exist in our wins.json at all.
holes_found.recommendation: Prefix dome labels with 'dome-WIN-' or equivalent. For example: 'SAA Decay Rate 77=77 nT/yr (dome-WIN-015, 20% tol)' with a single parenthetical at the list start: '(WIN-NNN here refers to the dome site\'s internal identifier in status_history.json source strings, not to our review\'s WIN entries.)'. Low-cost, pre-empts a confusing cross-reference trap.

holes_found.description: 'Domains 27 and 33' in the 'WIN-024 appears twice (domains 27 and 33)' sentence. The section counts only 28 scored domains, so domain #33 cannot be a scored-domain index. It presumably refers to the row position in the full 39-domain status_history.json list (which includes unscored / pending rows). Without that clarification, a defender can mock: 'They count 28 and cite domain 33 in the same paragraph — basic arithmetic failure.' Same risk for '(domains 27 and 33)' general phrasing.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '(domains 27 and 33)' with '(status_history.json row indices 27 and 33 of the 39-row snapshot; these correspond to the scored Roaring 40s Wind Speed and SAA-Roaring 40s Boundary entries)'. Add one sentence up top: 'Domain indices below refer to the row position in status_history.json (39 rows total, of which 28 are scored).'

holes_found.description: The assertion that H(r)'s parameters (8537, 8619) were 'fitted' is stated as fact: 'FORM-V51-001 has two free parameters (8537, 8619) which were fitted'. This is our conclusion, not self-evident. The dome author's public narrative is that 8537 km is a pressure-balance derivation and 8619 km is an aetheric scale length derived from first principles, not a data fit. If a dome defender points to a derivation page on the dome site, the 'fitted' claim is rebuttable.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to: 'FORM-V51-001 has two numerical parameters (8537, 8619). The dome author presents these as aetheric pressure/scale derivations from first principles; our Section 1.8 shows the values are only consistent with observational data when tuned — a post-hoc calibration in practical terms even if nominally theoretical.' Links the critique to existing Section 1.8 analysis instead of asserting 'fitted' bare.

holes_found.description: Selleri / Lorentz theory-framework dismissal is too strong. Section: 'Choosing between Selleri\'s absolute-simultaneity reformulation and Lorentz\'s relativity framework is a mathematical equivalence; GPS engineers obtain the same numbers either way. This is not a data test.' A defender can counter: the dome's choice of Selleri is not empty — Selleri / Lorentz are mathematically equivalent FOR SAGNAC and GPS clock offset, but they diverge in other contexts (e.g., interpretations of one-way vs two-way light speed measurements). Our blanket 'not a data test at all' is a strawman against the framework choice in principle.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to: 'Within the Sagnac and GPS clock-offset domains specifically, Selleri and Lorentz produce identical observables (both predict the conventional correction magnitudes); the dome dashboard labels this equivalence as a dome "pass". This is a framework-restatement pass rather than a data discrimination test — it does not distinguish the dome from the globe at the level of what these two instruments measure.' Preserves the point without the strawman flank.

holes_found.description: 'Pulsing green ring' rhetorical flourish may not match every reader's rendering of the live dashboard. If the dome author later redesigns the UI (static green vs animated), the section's visual description becomes a verifiable mismatch. Low stakes but easy to avoid.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'pulsing green ring reading "100.0%"' with 'prominent "100.0%" score graphic' — same semantic impact, no dependency on animation choice.

holes_found.description: Mascon Gravity '~26 vs 28.1 mGal' at 23% tolerance is classified under STATIC but is actually a range (~26 vs 28.1) with a non-trivial tolerance, and the predicted value is 'approximate' (~26). The status_history source string uses WIN-076 static win per live fetch — so the STATIC classification is defensible — but the mismatched-range framing (not equal-equal like the other STATIC entries) weakens the 'fixed number equalling itself' rhetoric for this specific line.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical: 'Mascon Gravity is the outlier in the STATIC bucket — predicted/observed are a range/point comparison rather than identical numbers; the source string "(WIN-076 static win)" still marks it as a re-read of the prior WIN value.'

holes_found.description: 'Tesla Harmonic Series — "Present" vs "Present", 0% tol. STATIC (WIN-001 static win). If the fundamental exists, its harmonics exist as arithmetic.' This dismissal is brittle. A defender can reply: 'Harmonics of a physical resonator are not pure arithmetic — their presence at given amplitudes tests cavity quality factor Q and damping, both falsifiable properties. Your claim is a strawman of resonance physics.' The 'arithmetic' framing is quickly rebuttable by any undergraduate physics student.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to: 'STATIC (WIN-001 static win). The monitor test records "Present" vs "Present" with 0% tolerance — a binary existence check, not a frequency-or-amplitude measurement. In physical resonance terms, harmonic existence at a detectable level is a Q-factor observation; this test does not record Q, amplitude, or linewidth, so the "pass" is a boolean re-read of WIN-001\'s fundamental, not a genuinely separate harmonic validation.'

holes_found.description: κ number inconsistency: Section writes 'κ = 1.672' in the Tesla-family header text ('driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672') AND 'κ = 1.67' in the list-item's TAUTOLOGY bullet ('κ = 1.67 is FORM-V51-004 itself'). FORM-V51-004 literally uses 1.672 (per formula_runtime.json we fetched). The dome's dashboard observed column lists predicted 1.67 nT/µGal — the dashboard has rounded to 1.67 while the underlying formula uses 1.672. This is a minor but verifiable imprecision on our part.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick one consistent value. Prefer the formula_runtime.json canonical 1.672 everywhere, with a parenthetical noting the dashboard rounds it to 1.67 ('FORM-V51-004 encodes κ = 1.672 exactly; the dashboard Predicted column rounds to 1.67').

holes_found.description: Section cites 'status_history.json 2026-04-17T18:31Z' but our live WebFetch of the current status_history.json returns most-recent entry at 2026-04-05T04:40:56Z. Either the dome has not updated status_history.json since then (in which case our 2026-04-17 citation is wrong), or the file structure includes per-day snapshots our fetch did not traverse. Either way, the cited timestamp is not directly verifiable and will age badly.
holes_found.recommendation: Change citation to 'status_history.json (snapshot of 2026-04-17, pulled by analyst via John09289/predictions repo commit at that time)' — flexible enough to survive dome file updates, still anchors the when. Alternatively, link to the commit SHA in the dome's docs/data directory that corresponds to the cited snapshot.

stronger_arguments.description: Dashboard-inflation ratio as a headline statistic. We make the 28:10 point in words but not as a quotable number. A single line like: 'At 28 scored domains for 10 independent numerical knobs, the dashboard has a 2.8× domain-inflation factor relative to its underlying parameter count. A well-normalized scorecard in physics (one knob per discriminating observation) has ratio 1.0×.' This is immediately quotable, travels well on social media, and frames the dome's dashboard as architecturally inflationary rather than merely individually-questionable.
sources: Our own Section 2b.4 cross-cutting analysis (if existing) for the one-ruler-many-rooms framing

stronger_arguments.description: Positive-feedback scoring architecture. Each new 'static win' entry added to monitor.py has predicted=observed hardcoded, which contributes +1/+1 to the pass ratio. The dashboard's 100.0% is structurally self-stable: adding any static entry maintains 100%. This is not a minor design flaw — it is a scoring architecture that cannot fail by construction. Explicitly pointing this out (a one-paragraph insert) is stronger than the current 'tautology' individual-bullet framing because it targets the scoring MECHANISM rather than individual cells.
sources: monitor.py source in john09289/predictions repo (see our Part 2b.1 Monitoring Illusion)
Our Section 2b.3 post-hoc retrodiction analysis — adjacent territory

stronger_arguments.description: Pre-registration asymmetry via OpenTimestamps canary. The dashboard's '100.0%' is anchored in status_history.json (which IS OTS-timestamped), while predictions live in monitor.py / model.html (which are only git-versioned). This is the exact Timestamp Error tab canary: the observations are timestamped, the predictions are not. Cross-linking from 2b.5 to the Timestamp Error tab would strengthen the section's verdict (dashboard headline is rhetorically anchored in OTS-signed bookkeeping, not in pre-registered predictions) and reinforce the canary across the site.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author's April-2026 additions of methodology.json and audit.html do explicitly name the bookkeeping / prospective-vs-retrospective distinction in plain language, which is unusually precise for a flat-earth site. The FORM-V51-001..006 formula_runtime.json is genuinely machine-readable and publicly inspectable — a transparency standard many mainstream science sites fall short of. Our audit uses the dome author's own exposed plumbing to make its point; this is reactive transparency working as designed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Exposing the plumbing does not normalize what flows through it. The author's disclosures state the audit is needed; they do not perform the audit. The headline '100.0% / 28 of 28' remains public, the bookkeeping caveat lives in auxiliary files most visitors never open, and the underlying formula-to-domain ratio (≈10 knobs to 28 labels) is inflationary regardless of how honestly the author documents it. Section 2b.5 is precisely the audit the author's own methodology says is owed.

deeper_analysis:
Overall assessment: the section is structurally sound and makes a non-trivial contribution beyond what Part 2b (code analysis) and Part 3 (prediction analysis) already cover. The EXP-172 analyst work is high quality — the domain_parameter_mapping_summary alone is a publishable supplementary table. The published HTML section, however, drops several of EXP-172's safety rails (explicit overlap disclosure, non-overlap bucket totals, the note about counts summing to 29 vs 28). Those safety rails exist in the source expansion JSON but were not promoted into the rendered prose, which creates the category-overlap and 5-vs-6 inconsistencies flagged above.

The κ tautology line (hole #1) is the only finding I rate as major. All others are moderate or minor and can be addressed with a single patch pass that adds ~15 sentences total. None of the holes undermine the section's core thesis — that the '100.0%' dashboard is label inflation on a 6-formula / 10-knob model — they refine the framing.

What a dome defender will attack first, ordered by likelihood:
(1) 'BOU 2017 is the same event used to fit κ' — factually wrong at first glance (two events, not one); easy rhetorical win for the defender if left unfixed. This is why I marked it major.
(2) '(5 domains)' header with 6 items — trivially observable; the kind of error screenshot-and-share bait.
(3) 'Domains 27 and 33' — the '28 scored but 33 is cited' arithmetic mismatch is a gift to someone looking for contradiction in our audit.
(4) Aetheric Redshift in both Borrowed AND Discriminating — self-contradiction of our own classification.
(5) Tesla Harmonic Series 'arithmetic' dismissal — any physics undergrad will pushback; fold into a quality-factor argument instead.

Credibility side: the 'Kernel of truth' paragraph correctly credits the author's methodology.json and audit.html as unusually precise for the flat-earth genre. This is the right framing. It should probably be hoisted higher in the section (currently last), because readers who bounce before the end miss the charity.

Advocate-mode check: if I were defending the dome, my sharpest counter to Section 2b.5 would be: 'The author never claimed the dashboard was a pre-registration ledger — he explicitly says it is monitoring. The review is attacking a claim the author did not make.' Our best response to that counter is already in the section's opening move (quoting methodology.json) — the frame is exactly right. The improvements above strengthen that frame; they do not change it.

https://john09289.github.io/predictions/data/status_history.json — WebFetch confirmed 28 passed / 28 scored / 39 total domains, overall_score 100.0, and source strings containing 'WIN-NNN static win' patterns for at least WIN-008, WIN-024, WIN-039, WIN-056, WIN-075, WIN-076.
Internal cross-ref: our WIN-012 detail_evidence confirms κ numerator (−10.9 nT BOU 2017) and denominator (−6.5 µGal Mohe 1997) geometry — this is the source of the major hole #1 correction.
citations_failed: status_history.json timestamp '2026-04-17T18:31Z' not directly verifiable by WebFetch — most-recent entry we retrieved was 2026-04-05T04:40:56Z. Either the cited snapshot is from a commit not reflected in the current served file, or the timestamp resolution differs. Flagged as minor hole.
citations_unchecked: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/audit.html — quoted text plausible, not re-verified this cycle. Would be a useful delta-check next cycle.

https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/methodology.json — quoted text plausible, not re-verified. Prior c2 review of the Timestamp Error tab verified methodology.json exists and contains the claim-taxonomy; the 'Display ratio is bookkeeping' line is consistent with that.
